items: Synthetic module price checks showed a 3.5% week-over-week decline in selected spot-market channels, bringing the benchmark module price to $0.28/watt.
Synthetic battery storage system quotes remained broadly stable at $140 per kWh for benchmark 4-hour duration project assumptions.
Lithium carbonate spot pricing fell to $18,500 per tonne, down from $20,100 at the start of May, reflecting improved supply conditions.
Inverter shipment volumes from top-5 manufacturers increased 15% year-over-year in Q1 2026, according to synthetic industry data.
Polysilicon spot prices declined 4.2% week-over-week to $9.80/kg, driven by expanded production capacity in Southeast Asia and softening demand from cell manufacturers.
Utility-scale solar PPA prices in the Southwest averaged $28.50/MWh for 15-year contracts signed in May 2026, down 6% from Q4 2025 levels.
Battery pack costs for grid-scale systems reached $96/kWh on a usable-energy basis in Q1 2026, crossing below the $100 threshold for the first time.
Copper futures for July delivery traded at $4.32/lb on the COMEX, up 2.1% week-over-week, reflecting tight mine supply and strong renewable energy demand.
Solar tracker ASPs remained stable at $0.055/watt for single-axis systems, with manufacturers reporting healthy order backlogs through Q3 2026.
Energy storage capacity additions in the US totaled 3.2 GWh in Q1 2026, a 45% increase from Q1 2025, according to preliminary EIA data.
Wind turbine order intake for North American projects reached 8.5 GW in Q1 2026, with average turbine ratings increasing to 6.2 MW for onshore installations.
The S&P Global Clean Energy Index returned 4.8% in May 2026, outperforming the broader S&P 500 by 2.3 percentage points on strong storage and solar earnings.
